business.headline: Dubai court acquits man in Dh1.6 million dispute, says failed deal isn't fraudDubai's criminal court acquitted a man accused in an investment-related dispute. The ruling said the case pointed to a civil business fallout, not proven criminal fraud.

Paytm UPI growing twice as fast as UPI industry, here's what's fuelling its comebackIn its Q4 FY26 earnings release filed last week, the company reported that consumer UPI gross transaction value (GTV) grew 46% year-on-year to Rs 5.5 lakh crore in the March quarter, more than double (2.2x) the industry's 21% growth rate.

India has 60 days of crude, no shortage anywhere, says Hardeep Singh PuriSpeaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Hardeep Singh Puri, said India's energy supplies remain secure despite geopolitical uncertainty and military tensions involving major oil-producing regions.

Retail inflation inches up to 3.48% in April, driven by rising food pricesData released by the government on Tuesday showed retail inflation, measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), rose to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March. The reading, however, came in lower than Reuters economists' expectations of 3.8%.

Uday Kotak warns consumers to 'prepare for the worst' amid US-Iran conflictSpeaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Kotak warned that rising energy prices linked to tensions in West Asia could soon begin hurting consumers and businesses, especially those already struggling with limited incomes.

Sensex ends 1,456 points lower, Nifty below 23,400; TCS, Infosys fall over 3%The BSE Sensex crashed 1,456.32 points or 1.92% to close at 74,558.96, while the NSE Nifty50 slumped 454.15 points or 1.91% to settle at 23,361.70.

Tourism body backs PM Modi's travel appeal, seeks more foreign touristsThe industry body said tourism can become a strong source of foreign exchange if the right steps are taken. It suggested easier visa rules, better flight connectivity and stronger global marketing to make India more attractive to international travellers.

Why Indians poured Rs 3 lakh crore into mutual funds despite market jittersIndia's mutual fund industry recorded total net inflows of Rs 3.22 lakh crore in April 2026, while overall Assets Under Management (AUM) climbed to a record Rs 81.92 lakh crore, according to the latest monthly data released by the Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI)..

Gold prices remain steady: Should you sell now or wait?With gold prices showing little movement in recent days, many investors are left wondering what to do next. Is this a pause before another rise, or a good moment to lock in profits and sell?

India declines US-sanctioned Russian LNG despite supply strainIndia has told Russia it will not buy liquefied natural gas cargoes covered by US sanctions. The decision has left a tanker waiting near Singapore and underlined the compliance risks around Russian LNG.

AI can improve portfolios, but good investing still needs context: Geojit's Jones GeorgeAs India's retail investing boom accelerates, AI-driven tools are reshaping how investors access markets and build portfolios. But technology alone cannot replace discipline, judgment and long-term financial understanding, says Jones George, Executive Director at Geojit Financial Services.

Groww shares tumble sharply after block deal: Should investors worry?Groww shares slid sharply after a block deal by early investors followed the lock-in expiry. Analysts said the fall reflected supply pressure and weak markets rather than any fresh business setback.

Sensex crashes over 1,500 points: Why is the stock market falling today?As of 3:13 pm, the Sensex was trading at 74,486.50, down 1,528.78 points or 2.01%, while the Nifty50 declined 458.85 points or 1.93% to 23,357.00.

Infosys, TCS shares tumble up to 4%: Why are IT shares falling today?Indian IT stocks slid sharply on Tuesday as global growth worries and broader market weakness intensified. The selloff underscored concerns over client spending, West Asia tensions and near-term volatility.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,800; Infosys, TCS down over 2%The BSE Sensex fell over 400 points in early trade to 75,611.60, down 403.68 points or 0.53% at 9:18 am. The NSE Nifty50 also slipped below the crucial 23,800 mark, trading at 23,703.65, down 112.20 points or 0.47%.

Rupee sinks to all-time low of 95.58 against US dollar amid crude oil shockThe rupee fell to a record 95.58 against the US dollar as crude prices jumped and Iran-related tensions flared. The slide has deepened concerns over inflation, market volatility and pressure on India's external balances.

PM Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year rattles jewellery marketsPrime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year has rattled jewellery markets, with traders warning that weaker demand could hurt small jewellers, karigars and thousands of workers dependent on the industry.
Zoho to bring back WFH? Sridhar Vembu responds to PM Modi's appealVembu said Zoho may revisit WFH after PM Modi urged fuel-saving measures to ease economic pressure.

Rs 17.3 lakh schooling, Rs 5.4 lpa salary: Is education becoming a financial trap?Education has long been treated as a non-negotiable expense in Indian households. But today, as costs rise year after year, more families are quietly asking a difficult question: is quality schooling becoming harder to afford?

Petrol, diesel prices may soon go up. How much more could you pay?Petrol and diesel prices could be hiked soon amid surging crude oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether the likely fuel price hikes would be sharp or gradual over a period of time.

entertainment.headline: Sharon Stone supports Sofía Vergara's take on dating rich men Sharon Stone reacted after an old interview of Sofía Vergara discussing dating standards resurfaced online.

Indian Idol fame Mohd Danish's Aigiri Nandini performance goes viral. Video Singer Mohd Danish of Indian Idol 12 fame has gone viral for singing Aigiri Nandini, a hymn dedicated to Goddess Durga. Viewers praised the emotional performance, saying it was a reminder that music goes beyond religion.

Who is Suraj Nambiar? Everything to know about Mouni Roy's husband Rumours about trouble in actor Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar's marriage gathered pace after they unfollowed each other on social media. With neither issuing a statement yet, online attention has shifted to who Suraj Nambiar is.

Mouni Roy archives wedding pics, husband Suraj deletes Insta account amid rift buzz Amid separation rumours, actor Mouni Roy has reportedly archived or removed several pictures with husband Suraj Nambiar, including their wedding photos. Suraj's Instagram account appears to have been deleted, too.

Kamal Haasan praises CM Vijay's move to shut liquor shops near temples and schools Kamal Haasan praised Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's first major decision after taking office, and the move is already drawing political attention across the state.

Vimal Khanna trailer out: Sunny Hinduja leads gritty survival thriller The trailer of Vimal Khanna is out, introducing Sunny Hinduja in a gritty survival thriller adapted from Surender Mohan Pathak's popular literary universe.

Akshay Kumar, Vidya Balan's untitled film with Anees Bazmee locks a release date Akshay Kumar, Vidya Balan and Anees Bazmee have reunited for an untitled family comedy due in cinemas in December this year. The film recently wrapped a major Kerala schedule and marks Sri Venkateswara Creations' 60th production.

Rajinikanth folds hands, avoids commenting on CM Vijay at Chennai airport. Video Rajinikanth did not respond when reporters at Chennai airport asked about Vijay becoming Tamil Nadu Chief Minister. The silence stood out because he had earlier congratulated Vijay's party on its election win.

Orry mocks Sara Ali Khan again, jokes about Rohit Shetty giving her a 'hit' Orry took a fresh swipe at Sara Ali Khan while speaking about Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 and Rohit Shetty. The remark has intensified chatter around their reported fallout and online bullying.

Work is more important: Amitabh Bachchan opens up about sleepless nights at 83 Amitabh Bachchan said his work commitments are causing sleepless nights despite advice to rest. He added music helped him find calm through long hours and late-night work.

Heer Sara Aur Pondicherry: Patralekhaa, Maanvi Gagroo explore friendship, freedom The trailer of Heer Sara Aur Pondicherry offers a glimpse into the journey of two women who set out on a life-changing trek of healing and self-discovery. Starring Maanvi Gagroo and Patralekhaa, the film explores friendship, freedom and breaking societal expectations.

Sebastian Stan confirms role in The Batman Part II, says film will blow people away Sebastian Stan confirmed his role in the upcoming superhero film The Batman: Part II. He said the Matt Reeves directorial is ambitious, demanding and could surprise audiences.

All about Sheema Kermani, Pasoori dancer and Pakistani feminist facing police action Viral videos of Pasoori dancer Sheema Kirmani being escorted by police in Karachi have sparked fresh conversations around activism, women's rights and artistic freedom in Pakistan.

Girls were harassed: Actor Vishal salutes CM Vijay's liquor shop crackdown Actor Vishal praised Chief Minister Vijay after he ordered the closure of 717 liquor shops near temples, schools and bus stands in Tamil Nadu. The move drew attention as Vijay's first major administrative decision, with Vishal highlighting concerns around shops near schools.

Still moping: Junaid Khan reveals father Aamir Khan's reaction to Ek Din failure Actor Junaid Khan said his recent release, Ek Din did not connect with most viewers despite the team's hopes. He added that his father, Aamir Khan, is still upset and takes such failures personally.

Who is Sanskruti Jayana, actor playing Satyabhama in Krishnavataram Part 1? Sanskruti Jayana has made her screen debut as Satyabhama in Krishnavataram Part 1: The Heart. Her family background, formal training and early praise have brought the newcomer into focus.

The Rivals of Amziah King trailer: Matthew McConaughey fights to save his family The first trailer for The Rivals of Amziah King shows Matthew McConaughey as a honey-maker and bluegrass musician facing rivals after reconnecting with his estranged foster daughter.

Satrangi trailer: Anshumaan Pushkar's Launda Naach act drives revenge drama The trailer of Satrangi - Badle Ka Khel promises an intense revenge drama set in rural Uttar Pradesh, starring Anshumaan Pushkar in a double role as Bablu and Lalli. The series also explores the traditional folk art of Launda Naach amid caste politics and family rivalry.

Priyanka Chopra shares adorable note given by daughter Malti Marie Priyanka Chopra Jonas shared a handmade Mother's Day note from daughter Malti Marie on social media. The note about her favourite things left the actor emotional as she wrote that her daughter knows her so well.

general news.headline: Nashik student arrested in NEET-UG case, paid Rs 10 lakh for leaked paperAs many as four teams of the Central Bureau of Investigation, which has been tasked with investigating the paper leak, have arrived in Nashik to take custody of the accused, Shubham Khairnar, who has been arrested by the Nashik Crime Branch.

Udhayanidhi Stalin repeats controversial comments on Sanatan, CM Vijay watchesUdhayanidhi Stalin repeated his call for the eradication of Sanatan Dharma in the Tamil Nadu Assembly. The remark drew fresh BJP attacks, while Assembly immunity limits the immediate legal risk.

After leaks, NTA gets into revenge mode to set tough question paper: Khan SirKhan Sir attacked the NTA after NEET-UG 2026 was cancelled over irregularity allegations. His remarks highlighted rising anger over student distress, paper leak claims and the wait for a re-exam.

Uday Kotak warns consumers to 'prepare for the worst' amid US-Iran conflictSpeaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Kotak warned that rising energy prices linked to tensions in West Asia could soon begin hurting consumers and businesses, especially those already struggling with limited incomes.

Dravid spends crores on a cricket team, but won't even attend practice. Here's whyRahul Dravid has been unveiled as Dublin Guardians owner in the European T20 Premier League. The move completes the six-team league structure ahead of the tournament's 2026 launch.

The NEET-UG paper 'leak' trail: Gurugram doctor, Kerala MBBS student, Sikar PGSikar has emerged as the main focus of the CBI's NEET paper 'leak' investigation. The alleged leak prompted the National Testing Agency (NTA) to cancel the NEET-UG exam held on May 3, affecting over 22 lakh candidates.

Meet Radhan Pandit Vettrivel, Vijay's astrologer who landed key government postIndian politicians, cutting across party lines, are known to have a strong belief in astrology. However, Vettrivel's earlier prediction that Vijay would have "tsunami-like strength" in politics highlights his confidence in the actor's rise.

Was offered money to target Virat Kohli: German influencer makes explosive claimIPL 2026: Virat Kohli's viral Instagram controversy has taken another twist after German influencer LizLaz claimed she was allegedly offered money to publicly target the RCB star. The influencer said she refused to make false allegations against Kohli despite being approached.

Sikar coaching ecosystem, linked to NEET UG paper leak, has a murky pastThe thread of the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak is connected to Rajasthan's Sikar, which has emerged as a "mini Kota". Sikar came under the spotlight after the NEET-UG 2024 controversy. Linked to several state-level exam scams and paper leaks, Sikar is now under the scanner.

NEET-UG cancelled: Super 30's Anand Kumar wants China-like crackdown after leakAnand Kumar called for strict action against coaching centres after NEET-UG 2026 was cancelled over alleged irregularities. The decision has triggered a re-test and a CBI probe into the examination process.

Bengali Shaadi Mein Abdulla DeewanaThe despondency of the Non-Resident Bengalis or NRBs doesn't get reflected in the celebrations in West Bengal. The state no longer sticks out like a special sore thumb.

India's sharp message to China on providing Pak support during Op SindoorIn a sharp swipe at China, India said 'responsible nations' should reflect on backing state sponsors of terror. The remark came as China publicly admitted to helping Pakistan during Operation Sindoor last year.

Mouni Roy archives wedding pics, husband Suraj deletes Insta account amid rift buzzAmid separation rumours, actor Mouni Roy has reportedly archived or removed several pictures with husband Suraj Nambiar, including their wedding photos. Suraj's Instagram account appears to have been deleted, too.

Woman, ex-boyfriend kill her new lover, dump body in sack; notebook cracks caseA man was allegedly killed by the ex-boyfriend of a woman he had met on social media. Investigators said a notebook provided the clue that helped unravel the case.

Vijay meets 'rebel' AIADMK leaders backing TVK ahead of crucial floor testThe rebel leaders, CV Shanmugam and SP Velumani, who enjoy the support of 30 MLAs, on Tuesday extended their support to the TVK for tomorrow's floor test.

An RSS whisper and beauty parlour didis: How BJP swept Bengal with micro campaignsWhile Narendra Modi and Amit Shah's rallies acted as booster shots in West Bengal, RSS affiliates and the BJP's booth-level workers carried out a year-long whisper campaign. They used auto drivers and beauty parlour didis as local influencers, and turned the anxiety and resentment around Mamata Banerjee's rule into a saffron tsunami for the BJP.

Alia Bhatt looks like a summer dream in first look from Cannes 2026Alia Bhatt is back in Cannes, and her first appearance this year is already serving major summer holiday fashion inspiration. She wore a rather special hand-painted dress as an ode to the French Riviera.
He's weak, Xi's not: Why Trump's China summit has allies on edgeTrump may offer China concessions at the expense of US allies — and to the detriment of his own country's interests.

Can 22 lakh NEET aspirants sue NTA over NEET-UG 2026 paper leak?The National Testing Agency (NTA) has cancelled the NEET-UG 2026 exam over a paper leak. Around 22 lakh aspirants who took the exam will have to sit for the MBBS entrance exam again. Can these 22 lakh students sue the NTA? Here's what legal experts say.

Four Indian tourists 'mysteriously' faint at Thailand cafe, one dies hours laterSecurity staff at the cafe in Phuket said that five Indians arrived there together around 11 pm on Friday. About 1.54 am, four of them reportedly lost consciousness one by one. They were rushed to the hospital, a Thai newspaper reported.

Is poha an unhealthy breakfast option? Sanjeev Kapoor says yesChef Sanjeev Kapoor recently spoke about why poha - despite being one of India's most popular comfort breakfasts - may come with certain nutritional concerns.

Iran threatens to enrich uranium to weapons grade if attacked by US, Israel againIran said it could enrich uranium to 90 per cent if the US, Israel strike again. Iran's nuclear programme is at the core of the dispute, particularly the fate of roughly 400 kilograms of uranium already enriched to 60 per cent purity, just below weapons-grade levels.

Vijay's big CM move: 717 liquor shops near religious places, schools shutVijay ordered the closure of 717 Tasmac liquor shops located within 500 metres of religious places, educational institutions and bus stands across Tamil Nadu within two weeks.

Kerala CM suspense: Ball in Rahul Gandhi's court, decision in next 24 hoursSources said the party is likely to take a final call on the next Kerala CM within the next 24 hours, with Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge expected in Delhi by evening to participate in the final discussions.

sports.headline: Ukraine's Elina Svitolina slams IOC over relaxation stance on Belarus athletes Elina Svitolina slammed the IOC for relaxing restrictions on Belarusian athletes, calling the move "sad and painful" while the war in Ukraine continues. The decision could allow Belarus athletes to compete under their national flag again.

Was offered money to target Virat Kohli: German influencer makes explosive claim IPL 2026: Virat Kohli's viral Instagram controversy has taken another twist after German influencer LizLaz claimed she was allegedly offered money to publicly target the RCB star. The influencer said she refused to make false allegations against Kohli despite being approached.

Mohammad Rizwan faces brutal trolling before bizarre dismissal in Mirpur Test: Watch BAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Mohammad Rizwan was brutally trolled by Litton Das, after which Nahid Rana dismissed him. Bangladesh sealed a historic 104-run victory in Mirpur to take a 1-0 lead and register their first-ever home Test win over Pakistan.

Man City still in Premier League title race: Guardiola unfazed by Arsenal win Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola said Arsenal's late win over West Ham United on Sunday had not added any extra pressure on his side as the Premier League title race heads into its decisive phase.

Hansi Flick agrees Barcelona contract extension until 2028 after La Liga glory Hansi Flick confirmed on Tuesday that he will extend his stay at FC Barcelona until 2028 after leading the Catalan giants to their 29th La Liga title and another dominant domestic campaign.

Virat Kohli weakness figured out? KKR coach wary despite RCB star's lean run IPL 2026: Shane Watson said KKR will still prepare closely for Virat Kohli ahead of the Raipur clash with RCB. His back-to-back ducks have not reduced the threat he poses as Bengaluru push for a top-two finish.

GT vs SRH LIVE: Hyderabad in trouble as Abhishek, Head depart early in 169 chase GT (Gujarat) vs SRH (Hyderabad) Live Score, IPL 2026: SRH (6/2 in 1.4 ovs). Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma have departed early as the Sunrisers Hyderabad are in early trouble while chasing 169.

Kolkata Knight Riders sweat over Varun Chakravarthy fitness ahead of RCB clash Shane Watson said Varun Chakravarthy is still being monitored before KKR's must-win game against RCB. The spinner's fitness matters to a late playoff push built on his sharp mid-season turnaround.

Sat-Chi put social media storm behind, begin Thailand Open on a winning note Thailand Open Super 500: Top seeds Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty beat Indonesia's Muh Putra Erwiansyah and Bagas Maulana in three games in a tense first-round match on Tuesday, May 12.

FIFA World Cup 2026 telecast in doubt, Delhi HC seeks Doordarshan response The Delhi High Court has asked the Centre and Prasar Bharati to respond to a plea on broadcasting the FIFA World Cup 2026 in India. The case brings renewed focus on public access as the tournament nears without a confirmed Indian broadcaster.

The perfect trigger: How Salman Agha fuelled Bangladesh's historic Test win vs Pakistan BAN vs PAK, 1st Test: On Monday, Salman Agha had challenged Bangladesh to set Pakistan a target of 260 in 70 overs. On Tuesday, Pakistan slumped to a 104-run defeat as the Tigers roared to a 1-0 series lead in Mirpur.

Cristiano Ronaldo's Al Nassr takes on Benzema's Al Hilal for Saudi league titleCristiano Ronaldo's Al Nassr host Karim Benzema's Al Hilal in Riyadh with the Saudi Pro League title on the line. Victory would end Al Nassr's seven-year wait and deliver Ronaldo his first league crown there.

Pakistan suffer humiliating first-ever Test defeat in BangladeshBangladesh created history in Dhaka on Tuesday, beating Pakistan at home for the first time with a 104-run win in the opening Test at the Shere Bangla National Stadium. The victory was also Bangladesh's third straight Test win over Pakistan.

MS Dhoni to play in Europe? Abhishek Bachchan reveals his big ETPL wishAbhishek Bachchan said he would love to see MS Dhoni play in the European T20 Premier League. His wish also exposed the BCCI rule that keeps contracted Indian players out of overseas leagues.

Virat Kohli jokes about twin ducks in hilarious dressing room spech: VideoVirat Kohli laughed off his recent batting slump with a cheeky dressing room joke after RCB's win over Mumbai Indians, showing little concern as Bengaluru push for a top-two IPL finish.

Dravid spends crores on a cricket team, but won't even attend practice. Here's whyRahul Dravid has been unveiled as Dublin Guardians owner in the European T20 Premier League. The move completes the six-team league structure ahead of the tournament's 2026 launch.

Shabnim reverses retirement, returns to South Africa Women's T20 World Cup squadSouth Africa have received a massive boost ahead of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 after veteran fast bowler Shabnim Ismail reversed her international retirement to return to the Proteas squad for the marquee tournament in England and Wales.

Sakshee Malikkh rallies behind Vinesh Phogat: Don't block a mother's right to competeOlympic medalist Sakshee Malikkh hit out at the WFI for stalling Vinesh Phogat's historic return to wrestling. Citing global support for female athletes, she urged the government to remove bureaucratic barriers against the new mother.

Wrestler's protest cost athletes international exposure: Deepak PuniaDeepak Punia said the disruption after the wrestlers' protest reduced overseas competition for Indian wrestlers. He said the fallout affected preparation, selection opportunities and long-term progress.

Ochoa's last World Cup mission? Mexican great targets Messi, Ronaldo milestoneVeteran Mexico goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa is on the verge of creating football history as the 40-year-old closes in on a sixth FIFA World Cup appearance in 2026, a rare feat achieved only by Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo.

Meet Rishabh Yadav: The boy who wants to give Indian archery its greatest dayRishabh Yadav has set his sights on LA 2028 after a breakout 2025 in compound archery. His rise, and his world-record pairing with Jyothi Surekha Vennam, has strengthened India's hope of an overdue Olympic medal.

Rahul Dravid backs bowler-friendly pitches to restore balance in T20sFormer India coach Rahul Dravid said T20 cricket needs more challenging pitches to give bowlers a fairer chance, warning that the format risks becoming too heavily skewed towards batters because of the dramatic evolution in modern-day hitting.

Does Virat Kohli fake aggression for cameras? Manjrekar offers ringside viewSanjay Manjrekar said he is surprised Virat Kohli still carries the same fire and energy at 37, adding that the star batter's emotional intensity and sensitivity to criticism have long fuelled his game.

Is Shreyas Iyer annoyed by PBKS' bowlers? Numbers and his reaction say soShreyas Iyer's blunt post-match reaction reflected Punjab Kings' growing bowling crisis, with PBKS conceding 200-plus totals seven times this IPL season despite staying in the playoff hunt.

French Open: Stan Wawrinka, Gael Monfils given wildcard entries in Roland GarrosFrench Open organisers have handed wildcard entries to Stan Wawrinka and Gael Monfils for Roland Garros, giving the two veteran stars one final chance to compete at a tournament that shaped their remarkable careers.

No Neeraj Chopra in Rome entry list, Sachin Yadav set for Diamond League debutNeeraj Chopra is set to miss the Rome Diamond League as Sachin Yadav prepares for his debut. Chopra's absence shifts focus to Yadav, who enters a stacked field after his Tokyo breakthrough.

IPL 2026: Axar Patel fined for slow over-rate after PBKS vs DCAxar Patel was fined Rs 12 lakh after Delhi Capitals maintained a slow over-rate against Punjab Kings in Dharamshala. The sanction came as Delhi kept their IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive with a three-wicket win.

IPL, GT vs SRH: Gujarat Titans host SRH as playoff race enters decisive phaseIPL, GT vs SRH: Third-placed Gujarat Titans host second-placed Sunrisers Hyderabad in a high-stakes IPL 2026 clash in Ahmedabad on Tuesday, with the playoff race heating up and the battle for a top-two finish gathering pace.

IPL playoff scenario: Punjab Kings enter danger zone, Delhi Capitals cling onDC vs PBKS: The race for the playoffs is heating up with every passing match. On Monday, Delhi Capitals somehow kept their qualification hopes alive with a thrilling three-wicket win over Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings in Dharamsala.

No mat, no mercy: Vinesh Phogat vs wrestling federation latest episode explainedThe dispute between Vinesh Phogat and the Wrestling Federation of India has evolved into a major governance battle. As both sides continue to defend their positions, the standoff has raised larger questions about how international clearances and domestic federation regulations intersect in Indian sport.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
